channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Denny Medley / Imagn Images / ReutersIn Kansas City, Missouri, fans cheer with Argentina’s Lionel Messi as he celebrates scoring the team’s first goal against Algeria at Kansas City Stadium on June 16, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Alfredo Estrella / AFP / GettyA fan of Team South Africa cheers for their team during the 2026 World Cup Group A football match agains Mexico at Mexico City Stadium on June 11, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Amanda Perobelli / ReutersIn Miami Gardens, Florida, a Team Uruguay fan cheers before the match against Saudi Arabia at Miami Stadium on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Mario Tama / GettyIn Los Angeles, fans of Team USA watching the match at the FIFA Fan Festival at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum cheer after the U.S. scored its first goal in the World Cup match against Paraguay on June 12, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Andre Penner / APFans of Team Iran smile for a photo before the World Cup Group G soccer match against New Zealand in Inglewood, California, on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Thomas Niedermueller / GettyFootball fans watch the World Cup Group E match between Germany and Curaçao in a church in Bad Cannstatt in Stuttgart, Germany on June 14, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Joel Marklund / Bildbyrån / ReutersFans of Argentina celebrate after the World Cup match between Argentina and Algeria on June 16, 2026, in Kansas City.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Phil Noble / ReutersA fan of Team Curaçao, seen inside Houston Stadium before a match against Team Germany, on June 14, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Kazi Salahuddin Razu / NurPhoto / GettyBangladeshi fans of Team Argentina watch the Group J match between Argentina and Algeria on a giant screen in Dhaka, Bangladesh, on June 17, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Tita Barros / ReutersBrazil fans react in front of a video screen on Copacabana beach in Rio de Janeiro, as they watch their team play against on Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium on June 13, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Bob Dechiara / Imagn Images / ReutersScotland’s Tartan Army marches to Boston’s Fenway Park for a Scottish celebration at a Red Sox game, a day after Team Scotland defeated Haiti in a Group C World Cup match at Boston Stadium, on June 14, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Ulises Ruiz / AFP / GettyIn Zapopan, Mexico, a fan of Team South Korea poses before the Group A match between South Korea and the Czech Republic at the Guadalajara Stadium on June 11, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Joosep Martinson / FIFA / GettyA Canada fan arrives ahead of the World Cup 2026 Group B match between Canada and Bosnia and Herzegovina at Toronto Stadium on June 12, 2026, in Toronto, Ontario.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Tom Fox / The Dallas Morning News / GettyIn Arlington, Texas, a sea of Netherlands fans gather around the double-decker Dutch Orange Bus as they prepare to leave from Choctaw Stadium and make their way to the match against Team Japan at Dallas Stadium on June 14, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Hannah McKay / ReutersIn Mexico City, Mexico, fans cheer for their team during a match against South Africa in Estadio Azteca on June 11, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Apu Gomes / AFP via GettyIn Los Angeles, Mexico fans at a watch party at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum celebrate a goal scored by Julián Quiñones during the match between Mexico and South Africa on opening day of the tournament, on June 11, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Paul Christian Gordon / ZUMA Press Wire / ReutersFans arrive for the FIFA World Cup match between Belgium and Egypt at Seattle Stadium on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Matt McNulty / FIFA / GettyIn Los Angeles, fans of Team USA celebrate after their team’s first goal during the Group D match against Paraguay, at Los Angeles Stadium, on June 12, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Quetzalli Nicte-Ha / ReutersA figurine of the baby Jesus is dressed in a jersey of the Mexican national team and arranged at the Metropolitan Cathedral, where fans visit and pray for their team to advance in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, in Mexico City, Mexico, on June 16, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Steph Chambers / FIFA / GettyIn Kansas City, Missouri, fans of Argentina, one of whom wears a llama costume, cheer outside Kansas City Stadium before the Group J match between Argentina and Algeria on June 16, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Charlotte Wilson / GettyIn Arlington, Texas, Japan fans cheer in the stands during the Group F match between Netherlands and Japan at Dallas Stadium on June 14, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Talip Demirci / Anadolu / GettyAn aerial view of football fans gathered to watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup Group D match between Australia and Turkey on a giant screen in the 2,000-year-old historic Antiphellos theater, in the Kas district of Antalya, Turkey, on June 14, 2026. The actual match took place at the BC Place Stadium in Vancouver, Canada.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Passion of World Cup Fans
item.description:
Daniel Ramalho / Xinhua / GettyA fan cheers before a press conference announcing Brazil’s 26-player squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, on May 18, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Druzhkivka, Ukraine — Firefighters take a moment to rest inside a residential building set alight by a Russian drone attack. — Photograph: Diego Herrera Carcedo/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
West Bank — Two Palestinian women carry a sofa past an Israeli soldier after being allowed to retrieve their belongings from the Nur Shams refugee camp, where many homes have been destroyed by the IDF. — Photograph: AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tyre, Lebanon — A young girl surveys the aftermath of Israeli strikes from a broken window in Jabal Amel hospital, where she has taken shelter with her family. — Photograph: Zohra Bensemra/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Bucharest, Romania — Education workers shelter from the sun during a protest against planned changes to salaries for public sector workers. — Photograph: Alexandru Dobre/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Paz, Bolivia — Trucks queue for fuel after more than 40 days of road blockades by protesters demanding economic reform and the resignation of President Rodrigo Paz. — Photograph: Jorge Mateo Romay Salinas/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caracas, Venezuela — Power cables crisscross the street in a country that has just agreed a deal with the US company General Electric to address its struggling energy infrastructure. — Photograph: Federico Parra/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] AI arts and gold-mining in mud: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tirana, Albania — Protesters object to a proposed luxury tourism development on the 17th consecutive day of protests outside the prime minister’s office. — Photograph: Vlasov Sulaj/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Bitlis, Turkey — A shepherd drives his sheep through a dust cloud towards the pastures of Mount Nemrut, where the flock will spend the summer. — Photograph: Mustafa Kilic/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York City, US — New York Knicks fans celebrate after they against San Antonio Spurs. The Knicks staged the in history to overcome the Spurs in the dying seconds – and put themselves 3-1 up and within one game of a rare championship win. — Photograph: Adam Gray/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
San Antonio, US — Victor Wembanyama of the San Antonio Spurs shoots the ball against Karl-Anthony Towns and OG Anunoby of the New York Knicks during the third quarter in Game 2 of the at Frost Bank Center. — Photograph: Eric Gay/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A humanoid traffic officer and Pride in Brazil: photos of the day – Monday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kathmandu, Nepal — Monkeys drink water from taps at the peace pond at Swayambhunath Stupa, a Buddhist monument and Unesco world heritage site. — Photograph: Narendra Shrestha/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
St John’s, Canada — A medium-size iceberg floats in the Atlantic near Signal Hill off the coast of St John’s. Icebergs break off from Baffin Island and Greenland every spring and drift down the stretch of water along the coast of Newfoundland and Labrador known as ‘Iceberg Alley’. — Photograph: Ken Cedeno/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Barcelona, Spain — Clergy attend the inauguration of the Tower of Jesus Christ at the Basilica of the Sagrada Família. One hundred and forty-four years after work began, of Antoni Gaudí’s cathedral in the presence of members of the Spanish royal family, the prime minister and hundreds of bishops. — Photograph: Emilio Morenatti/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Manta, Ecuador — Fishing boats burn in the port city. At least 35 fishing boats were destroyed and two people were injured in a fire that broke out at a crowded dock in western. — Photograph: Ariel Ochoa/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nanyuki, Kenya — A protester is arrested by anti-riot police during the protest against the proposed Ebola quarantine centre. partly on the grounds that they do not want potential carriers of the highly contagious disease on Kenyan soil. — Photograph: Brian Inganga/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nanyuki, Kenya — Demonstrators run for cover during clashes with Kenyan police officers at a protest against a controversial quarantine facility for US citizens which is slated to begin operations at Laikipia airbase in Nanyuki. during the protest. Reporters from Reuters saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound. — Photograph: Luis Tato/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Berlin, Germany — Nurses Louise and Ursula spray each other with a disinfectant foam while demonstrating protocols for dealing with infected Ebola patients at Charité hospital in Berlin. The hospital recently treated the American doctor Peter Stafford, who survived the infection that he contracted in the. — Photograph: Sean Gallup/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Bunia, Democratic Republic of the Congo — Members of a Congolese Red Cross team carry the body of a woman suspected of having died from , placing it in a coffin before her safe burial at her home in the city. could spread to be similar in scale to the worst outbreak in history, that killed more than 11,000 people, by US health officials. — Photograph: Jospin Mwisha/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Calcutta, South Africa — An indoor residual spraying technician fumigates a resident’s home as part of a elimination effort. Human-induced climate change, compounded by the La Niña weather pattern, has intensified extreme rainfall across southern Africa, creating conditions that can fuel malaria transmission long after flood waters recede. — Photograph: Emmanuel Croset/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Donetsk, Ukraine — Service members of the 93rd Mechanized Brigade fire a rocket from a multiple rocket launcher toward Russian positions. The military situation is the most promising it has been for Kyiv for two and a half years, said : ‘We can’t say Russia is losing this war. But we can say they are losing the initiative each day, day by day.’. — Photograph: 93rd Mechanized Brigade/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Newark, US — A protester waves an upside-down American flag, a traditional symbol of distress, outside the Delaney Hall detention centre. The facility, run by the private prison company Geo Group, has in recent weeks. — Photograph: Andrés Kudacki/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Newark, US — A detainee waves from inside Delaney Hall detention centre during a protest over conditions inside the facility in New Jersey. Dozens of detained women announced. — Photograph: Andrés Kudacki/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Belfast, Northern Ireland — A resident looks at the charred debris scattered on the street in her neighbourhood, in the aftermath of anti-immigrant protests. and blocked roads in and around Belfast this week, after Elon Musk, Tommy Robinson and other agitators exhorted people to take to the streets. — Photograph: Isabel Infantes/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Belfast, Northern Ireland — Protesters set fires during disorder on Antrim Road in Newtownabbey. Protests against immigration erupted into violence after far-right activists called for demonstrations in response that was captured in a graphic video. A 30-year-old Sudanese national has been charged with attempted murder following the knife attack. — Photograph: Adam Vaughan/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Jericho, West Bank — Israeli settlers look at a fallen rocket outside a Jewish settler farm on the outskirts of the Israeli-occupied city, following Iranian and Iran-backed Houthi rebel attacks. at a time when the conflict is becoming . The president faces , rising inflation and plummeting approval ratings. — Photograph: Ilia Yefimovich/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Dahiyeh, Lebanon — People check destroyed apartments that were hit by an Israeli airstrike in a southern suburb of Beirut. Iran said this week that . Israeli strikes there have reportedly killed more than 3,600 people, while Hezbollah attacks have killed at least 30 Israeli soldiers in Lebanon and Israeli civilians. — Photograph: Hassan Ammar/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Umm al-Khair, West Bank — Palestinian boys look toward a young Israeli settler after their football, kicked beyond a barbed-wire fence near the Carmel settlement, was taken away. The UK, in alliance with a group of other western powers including Australia, France and Norway, has announced involved in enabling and financing the recent upsurge in settler violence in the. — Photograph: Léo Corrêa/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Basilica Blessing, Tree Tunnel, Parrot Prediction
item.description:
Sebastian Willnow / DPA / GettyA blue-throated macaw sits in front of a video camera at Leipzig Zoo on June 9, 2026. In the zoo aviary, parrots were asked to act as oracles for the opening World Cup match between Mexico and South Africa, picking the winner. One ended up grabbing the ball marked “Mexico” from a box with its beak.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography
